text_template: Base rent starts at {base_rent} per month with annual escalation of {escalation_rate}%.

rewrite_options.description: Cap annual rent escalation at 2%
Allow step-up schedule with one month free rent in year one
Fix rent for first two years before escalation begins

link_effect: Reducing rent pressure often leads to stricter maintenance allocation and tighter subletting economics.

text_template: Tenant pays common area maintenance at {cam_model} with reconciliation every {reconcile_period}.

rewrite_options.description: Cap annual CAM increase at 4%
Add annual CAM audit rights for tenant

text_template: Tenant is responsible for all interior and structural repairs above {repair_threshold} per incident.

rewrite_options.description: Landlord remains responsible for structural and roof systems
Raise tenant repair threshold to $2,500
Share major HVAC replacement costs 50/50

link_effect: Shifting maintenance burden back to landlord can trigger demands for stronger rent escalations.

text_template: Landlord maintenance response is commercially reasonable, typically within {response_window} business days.

rewrite_options.description: Require response within 2 business days
Require 24-hour response for emergencies

text_template: Tenant principal must provide a personal guarantee for {guarantee_duration} and maintain a security deposit of {security_deposit}.

rewrite_options.description: Convert to burn-off guarantee ending after 12 on-time months
Reduce security deposit to 2 months rent
Replace personal guarantee with letter of credit

link_effect: Reducing guarantee protections typically causes stricter termination penalties or higher insurance/security requirements.

text_template: Failure to replenish deposit within {replenish_days} days is a lease default.

rewrite_options.description: Allow 15 days to replenish deposit
Require written notice and cure right before default

text_template: Subletting requires landlord consent, not to be unreasonably withheld, and landlord receives {recapture_share}% of excess subrent.

rewrite_options.description: Limit landlord recapture to 25% of excess subrent
Require landlord response to sublet requests within 10 business days
Pre-approve transfers to affiliates meeting financial criteria

link_effect: Broader subletting rights commonly lead to stronger rent escalation demands.

text_template: Any assignment triggers an administrative fee of {assignment_fee} and landlord legal costs.

rewrite_options.description: Cap assignment fee at $1,000
Allow only documented actual legal and processing costs

text_template: Early termination requires payment of {termination_penalty} plus unamortized tenant improvement costs.

rewrite_options.description: Limit early termination penalty to 6 months rent
Use a tapered penalty that declines each lease year
Allow release if tenant secures a replacement tenant

link_effect: Reducing guarantee protections leads landlords to tighten early termination economics.

text_template: Landlord may terminate for redevelopment on {redevelopment_notice} days notice with relocation at tenant cost.

rewrite_options.description: Require 120 days redevelopment notice
Require landlord to cover documented relocation costs

text_template: Tenant must maintain general liability insurance of {liability_limit} and property coverage for all improvements.

rewrite_options.description: Set liability insurance requirement at $2M
Tie insurance levels to market-standard coverage for similar tenants

text_template: Tenant indemnifies landlord for all claims except those arising from landlord {carveout_scope}.

rewrite_options.description: Carve out landlord negligence from tenant indemnity
Convert to mutual indemnity based on each party's fault
Limit indemnity to third-party claims only

link_effect: If insurance obligations are reduced, landlord typically demands stronger guarantee or higher deposit protections.
